design_fidelity.note: Brief specifies a LIGHT palette (#FFFFFF bg, #111827 fg, #3B82F6 primary). The code ships a charcoal-by-default palette. The LLM caps palette at 2/10 for the inverted scheme, dropping overall below the pass threshold.

missing_features: The dark-mode toggle is a primary feature, not a nice-to-have
feature_coverage.note: Both Stage 1 and the LLM judge identify the missing toggle. The LLM picks up the JS comment that admits the implementation was skipped, so no rubber-stamp override is needed for the case to FAIL.

rationale: Two layers fail. `design_fidelity` reports overall 4/10 with palette weak at 2/10 — the brief asks for a light palette but the code ships dark by default. `feature_coverage` reports the `light/dark mode toggle` feature missing: Stage 1 finds no occurrences of `toggle`, `light`, or `dark` in the code blob; the LLM independently identifies the absent toggle UI (citing the JS comment that admits 'no theme button was added'). `llm_overrides` is 0 — the rubber-stamp guard is in place but did not need to fire because the model correctly identified the gap.
